confirmed: The Fauna candidate-lane README existed at the pinned base and was updated in place.
The bounded repository search surfaced the parent README but no child Fauna candidate dossier.
The domain-fauna workflow checks that this lane contains no non-README candidate record and retains explicit validation, proof, and release-dry-run holds.
The Fauna release index is a draft human navigation surface whose identifiers and release-state forms remain illustrative, proposed, or conflicted rather than verified release instances.
Fauna sensitivity doctrine is deny-by-default and deliberately excludes exact locations and operational transform parameters from public documentation.
The Fauna domain and sensitivity policy READMEs are scaffolds and do not establish executable policy enforcement.
The Fauna proof lane is a draft guide; concrete proof inventory, producers, access controls, and release linkage remain unverified.
The Fauna test lane is a scaffold and sampled direct test modules contain only PROPOSED placeholder docstrings.
The published Fauna README defines a release-gated public-safe carrier boundary while emitted release-linked artifacts remain unverified.
CODEOWNERS routes release and Fauna documentation review to bartytime4life while explicitly denying that routing is stewardship, review completion, release approval, or publication authority.
proposed: release/candidates/fauna/ should operate as a repository-grounded, deny-by-default Fauna candidate index and pre-publication review contract without becoming taxonomic, source, evidence, policy, stewardship, legal, release, or publication authority.

rollback.strategy: Close the draft pull request before merge, or revert the generated-receipt commit and README commit in reverse order after merge.
